evals.name: audit-prominence-over-chronology
evals.prompt: Audit my consultant profile. It is currently a reverse-chronological project list, and I worry that older recognizable clients in regulated banking and telecommunications are buried under newer but less relevant projects.
evals.expected: A profile thesis, prominence map, proposed structure, rewrite samples, and factual gaps. The answer should recommend selected highlights based on signal strength rather than chronology alone.

evals.name: rewrite-website-bio-for-buyers
evals.prompt: Rewrite this senior frontend/product engineering consultant bio for my website. I want buyers to understand my industries, client types, and what I am good at within 30 seconds.
evals.expected: A website-suitable consultant profile with headline, executive summary, capability pillars, selected highlights, additional projects, and concise skills/tooling.

evals.name: compress-projects-to-one-page-cv
evals.prompt: Turn these 18 project entries into a strong one-page consultant CV. Keep it truthful, do not invent metrics, but make the strongest client and impact signals visible.
evals.expected: A compact one-page structure that preserves chronology where useful, adds a selected-impact or selected-client signal area, and rewrites bullets with action, scope, and qualified impact.

evals.name: localize-profile-for-dach-and-linkedin
evals.prompt: I need German and English versions of my consultant profile for DACH clients, international EU leads, and LinkedIn. Do not just translate it literally; adapt tone and structure.
evals.expected: A localization plan with DACH/German, EU/international English, and LinkedIn variants, including headline/About examples, terminology choices, and notes on what facts must stay aligned.

evals.name: sync-linkedin-with-pdf-profile
evals.prompt: Review my LinkedIn profile against my PDF consultant profile. I want LinkedIn to stay updated from the same project data, but I am not sure what should be copied, shortened, or posted separately.
evals.expected: A channel comparison and sync plan covering LinkedIn headline, About, Featured, Experience, skills, recommendations, activity, source-of-truth data, and API/platform caution.

evals.name: strengthen-tone-without-invention
evals.prompt: This profile is factually right but feels too flat. Make the strengths actually read as strengths, but do not invent anything or turn it into hollow marketing copy.
evals.expected: A language strategy plus rewrites that use positive-but-earned framing, proof-backed claims, DACH and English tone variants, and a checklist of unsupported claims to avoid.

evals.name: translate-german-profile-idiomatically
evals.prompt: Translate my German Beraterprofil into natural English for LinkedIn and an international PDF. Keep the facts aligned, but make the English sound like a real person wrote it, not like a German CV was translated sentence by sentence.
evals.expected: Localized English variants for LinkedIn and PDF, with idiomatic phrasing, first-person LinkedIn tone where appropriate, proof-backed positive claims, and notes on German concepts that should not be translated literally.

evals.name: find-professional-through-line
evals.prompt: My project list reads like disconnected events. Please find the professional story across the projects: early frontend work, later enterprise clients, internationalization, and product engineering. Keep it concise and factual, not like a biography.
evals.expected: A profile audit that identifies a professional through-line, connects earlier and later projects, proposes where to place the narrative in the profile, and rewrites selected project entries so they support the arc without becoming dramatic or invented.

evals.name: run-completeness-interview-pass
evals.prompt: Run an interview/completeness pass before rewriting my consultant profile. The current homepage misses older projects that exist on the old homepage and LinkedIn, and I also have LinkedIn recommendations that may contain useful signals but should not be pasted into the profile.
evals.expected: A source inventory, project completeness table, prioritized interview questions, reference-signal map, and a recommendation for what can be safely improved now versus what needs answers or permission.

evals.name: translate-personality-into-professional-language
evals.prompt: I want my consultant profile to show more personality without becoming unprofessional. Earlier I used phrases like 'Nein-Sager' because I value clear boundaries, saying what I can and cannot do, design judgment, working well with designers, and being a self-taught engineer from a webdesign background.
evals.expected: A personality and boundaries strategy that translates raw labels into professional language, identifies where personality belongs by channel, proposes a working-principles block, keeps the design/autodidact story tied to proof, and warns against wording that sounds combative or unsupported.

evals.name: write-from-buyer-interviewer-perspective
evals.prompt: My coach says people buy from people. I do not want anonymous consultant profiles anymore. Rewrite my profile direction from the perspective of an interviewer who wants to understand what kind of person I am, what I enjoy, and what I want to achieve, while still keeping it professional and evidence-based.
evals.expected: A buyer/interviewer perspective pass that treats personality as part of trust and fit, adds a concise human-fit or motivation section, keeps proof and capability prominent, and avoids drifting into private biography or unsupported personal branding.

evals.name: do-not-inject-maintainer-client-history
evals.prompt: Rewrite my independent consultant profile from the project evidence I supplied. I have worked with regional healthcare and logistics companies, but no globally recognizable enterprise brands.
evals.expected: Uses only the supplied healthcare and logistics evidence, does not introduce example clients from the skill author or another consultant, and treats unnamed-client confidentiality as a normal constraint rather than a credibility gap.
